The vulnerability was due to the home blueprint being exempted from CSRF protection. This could allow modification of session query data in the context of the authenticated user. The issue was fixed by enabling CSRF protection for the affected blueprint and hardening query parsing. As reported by Bilal Teke.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00185, "percentile": 0.08176}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-j4rh-7jcr-qm69", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-06T22:31:39Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-j4rh-7jcr-qm69", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-j4rh-7jcr-qm69"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44364"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-13T20:16:23Z", "published_at": "2026-05-06T22:31:39Z", "references": ["https://github.com/MISP/misp-modules/security/advisories/GHSA-j4rh-7jcr-qm69", "https://github.com/MISP/misp-modules/commit/52cda9caa003cafe87e14ae3721db5e16f6f111a", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44364", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-j4rh-7jcr-qm69"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/MISP/misp-modules/security-advisories/GHSA-j4rh-7jcr-qm69", "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/MISP/misp-modules", "summary": "misp-modules website - Missing CSRF protection in the website home blueprint", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-14T20:42:31Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-j4rh-7jcr-qm69", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "misp-modules"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 3.0.7"}], "withdrawn_at": null}
